name: Tomato Lentil Stew Over Quinoa
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-large saucepan, bring water to a boil and add the lentils. Cook for about 30 - 40 minutes (or until thoroughly cooked).
2. While the lentils are cooking, chop onions and tomatoes.
3. In a very large skillet or pan, sauté onions over low heat until they are mostly translucent.
4. Add tomatoes to the skillet and stir occasionally.
5. Crush the marjoram and savory in your palm and rub together to release the flavor. Add them to the pan with the salt (3/4 teaspoon).
6. When the tomatoes appear stewy and cooked, add the cooked, well-drained lentils (which should be done by now). Stir for a few minutes to combine.
7. To cook quinoa, bring 2 parts water to 1 part quinoa in a separate pot. Add the grain and cook until tender (no need to rinse).
8. Stir and let sit covered off the heat for ten minutes before serving.
9. For an extra touch, steam cauliflower and add cooked arame seaweed on top. To prepare arame, soak a handful in cold water. Heat oil in a small pan, add garlic (optional) and ginger. Add the drained arame and sauté until crispy. Add soy sauce to taste.
10. Sprinkle the arame mixture over the stew or pasta dish before serving.
